steps.title: Compare value and perception
steps.content: The passage first states that volunteering helps society as a whole. The survey then shows most young people see only individual-level help, while just 6% connect volunteering with fixing broader social problems.

steps.title: Reject unsupported conclusions
steps.content: Choice A concerns where people can volunteer, not what they believe volunteering does. Choice C compares individual and societal benefit in a way the passage does not. Choice D concerns finding opportunities, not perceived impact. The survey suggests young people underestimate volunteering's usefulness. The answer is B.
